candidates.target: SAXPY arrays x/y/z and scalar a in the memory-bandwidth-bound loop
candidates.rationale: Profiling/telemetry indicate a memory-bound SAXPY (memory_bound_score=0.85, low arithmetic intensity). Retrieved pattern evidence recommends reducing 64-bit double usage when precision allows to cut cache pressure and memory traffic, improving cache miss behavior and effective bandwidth.
candidates.action_sketch: If numerical requirements permit, change x/y/z from double to float (and a to float), keep checksum accumulation in double to reduce validation noise; rebuild with -O3 and verify vectorization remains enabled.
preconditions: Application accuracy requirements allow single precision for x/y/z (or a mixed-precision approach) within an agreed tolerance
aligned_alloc alignment remains valid for the new element size (keep 64B alignment)
No external ABI/IO contract requires double arrays
correctness_checks: Compare checksum (or L2 norm / max-abs error) against double-baseline with tolerance appropriate to float (e.g., relative error <= 1e-5 to 1e-6 depending on use case)
Ensure no NaNs/Infs introduced and results are stable across reps
rollback_criteria: If error exceeds tolerance vs double-baseline
If runtime does not improve (or regresses) due to loss of vectorization/alignment issues
